issues.summary: [CP Improvements] Creative Preview 2026 Unified First Screen — Design Fixes
issues.description:
## Summary

Configure alerting rules for microsite frontend health metrics. Alerts should notify the team when error rates spike, page loads are consistently slow, or video playback is degraded — enabling proactive response before campaigns are impacted.

## Context

* Metrics flow: TV → metrics.js → telemetry endpoint → Kafka → metrics store → Grafana/Alertmanager
* Alerts should fire to Slack (non-critical) and PagerDuty (critical, if SLA-impacting)
* Microsites serve ads on Samsung TVs — a broken microsite = lost revenue + bad advertiser experience
* Reference dashboard: "Microsite Frontend Health" (separate task)

## Acceptance Criteria

### Critical Alerts (Slack + PagerDuty)

* \[ \] **High error rate**: JS error rate > 10% of page loads over 15min window
* \[ \] **Video playback failure**: Video error rate > 5% over 15min window
* \[ \] **Complete outage**: Zero metrics received for > 10min (when expected traffic > 0)

## Context

The `Immersive Masthead 2026` preview in Creative Preview tool has several visual discrepancies compared to the updated design spec.

**Component:** `immersive-masthead-2026-preview.tsx`

## Issues to Fix

### 1. Gradient is too strong and behaves incorrectly

* Current: `bottom-dim.png` and `left-dim.png` overlays are too intense
* Expected: Subtler gradient that matches the updated Figma design
* The gradient should transition/animate differently during fullscreen expansion

**Files:** `BottomDim`, `LeftDim` styled components + asset PNGs (`bottom-dim.png`, `left-dim.png`)

### 2. Missing blur on top section selector

* Current: `top-menu-focus.png` has no blur effect
* Expected: Blur/frosted glass effect on the top section selector when focused

**Files:** `TopMenuImage` component, possibly needs CSS `backdrop-filter: blur()` or updated asset (`top-menu-focus.png`)

### 3. Incorrect apps displayed

* Current: `apps.png` shows outdated set of applications
* Expected: Updated app icons matching the 2026 design (new apps lineup)

**Files:** `apps.png` asset needs replacement

### 4. Incorrect background color

* Current: `background-2026.png` has wrong color tone
* Expected: Updated background color per design spec

**Files:** `background-2026.png` (and possibly `background-2026-expanded.png`) need replacement

## Acceptance Criteria

* \[ \] Gradient overlays match Figma design — correct opacity, direction, and animation behavior
* \[ \] Top section selector has blur/frosted glass effect when focused
* \[ \] Apps row shows correct 2026 app lineup
* \[ \] Background color matches design spec
* \[ \] All states work correctly: default view, focused (top section selected), fullscreen expanded, fullscreen video play

## Technical Notes

* Assets to update: `bottom-dim.png`, `left-dim.png`, `top-menu-focus.png`, `apps.png`, `background-2026.png`, `background-2026-expanded.png`
* May need CSS changes for blur effect (`backdrop-filter`) instead of/in addition to asset replacement
* Gradient behavior during transitions controlled by styled-components props: `$isFullScreen`, `$fullScreenExpanded`

issues.summary: [Microsite UCS] Kafka consumer processor for microsite transcoding callbacks
issues.description:
### Description

Add a new processor `Microsites::Ucs::TranscodingProcessor` to handle UCS transcoding callbacks for microsites on the existing Kafka topic (`sdsp.ucs.stream`).

### Acceptance Criteria

* `Microsites::Ucs::TranscodingProcessor` registered in `MessageProcessor::PROCESSORS` under key `microsite_video`
* On `status: "transcoded"`: saves video variants to `MicrositeVideo.variants` (jsonb), extracts poster frame URL, transitions `processing_status` to `:ready`
* On `status: "error"`: transitions `MicrositeVideo.processing_status` to `:failed`, microsite to `:partially_failed`
* When ALL microsite videos are `:ready` → microsite `processing_status` → `:ready`
* When ANY microsite video is `:failed` → microsite `processing_status` → `:partially_failed`
* Triggers HTML generation task when microsite reaches `:ready`
* Handles `RecordNotFound` gracefully (logs warning, skips)
* Migration: `add_column :microsite_videos, :poster_frame_url, :string`
* Unit tests

### Technical Notes

* Correlation: `MicrositeVideo.find_by(video_asset_id: msg['original_asset_id'])`
* Kafka message (after `deep_transform_keys(&:underscore)`):

    ```
    {
      "ad_tag_id": "...",
      "status": "transcoded",
      "original_asset_id": "{asset_id}",
      "ad_type": "microsite_video",
      "metadata": { "data": [
        { "url": "...", "width": 1920, "height": 1080, "video_bit_rate": 5400, "asset_type": "video" },
        { "url": "...", "asset_type": "Image" }
      ]}
    }
    ```

## BUSINESS IMPACT

The Microsite section currently lives inside the DSP shell (`rtb-trader/ui/dsp/src/pages/microsites/`). As the feature grows in complexity (form builder, preview panel, listing, backend integration, asset uploads), keeping it in the DSP shell creates deployment coupling — any microsite change requires a full DSP shell deploy, and vice versa. Extracting microsites into their own micro frontend (MFE) enables independent deployments, faster CI/CD, smaller bundle sizes, and team autonomy — matching the established pattern used by `creatives-management`, `planning`, `sellers-ui`, `supply`, and `targeting` realms.

## USER STORY

As a developer working on the Microsite feature, I want the microsite UI to be a standalone micro frontend with its own build, deploy, and hosting infrastructure, so that I can develop and deploy microsite changes independently without coupling to the DSP shell release cycle.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

* New workspace `rtb-trader/ui/microsites-management/` created in the monorepo
* Module Federation configured — exposes `remoteEntry.js` with microsite routes
* DSP shell (`rtb-trader/ui/dsp`) consumes the new MFE as a remote
* All needed pages/components migrated from `rtb-trader/ui/dsp/src/pages/microsites/` to the new workspace
* Independent build and dev server works (`pnpm dev` in the new workspace)
* GHA workflow for deploying to S3 (staging + prod)
* Terraform configuration in `module-federation-ui-tf` for the new realm (S3 + CloudFront + WAF)

## BUSINESS IMPACT

Video is the highest-engagement creative format on Samsung TV. Microsites with video content see significantly higher dwell time and interaction rates compared to static-only microsites. This section allows advertisers to add up to 3 auto-playing videos in a loop, configure third-party video tracking (quartile progress events), and add click trackers — all essential for campaign measurement and optimization. Without proper video tracking, advertisers cannot measure video completion rates, which is a standard industry requirement.

## USER STORY

As a user creating a microsite with video, I want to upload up to 3 videos that auto-play in a loop, configure click trackers, and select which video progress events to track (start, quartiles, complete), so that I can create an engaging video experience with full measurement coverage.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

### Section Shell & Tabs

* Collapsible "Videos" section (visible only when Video toggle is ON)
* Header: "The videos will auto-play in order in a loop. You can add up to **3** videos."
* Tab bar: "1st Video" active by default, "+ Video" button to add tabs (max 3)
* Tabs labeled: "1st Video", "2nd Video", "3rd Video"
* Each tab (except last remaining) can be removed
* Switching tabs shows the corresponding video form

### Single Video Form

* **Video File** — radio group with help icon (?):

    * ● Upload Hosted Video (default) → drag-and-drop area (MP4, MOV)
    * ○ Upload VAST URL → text input
    
* **Video thumbnail** — info banner: "Video thumbnail available only when 2 or more videos selected."
* **Impression Tracker** — dynamic list with "+ Add" button, each with text input + ✕ remove

### Video Third Party Trackers

* Checkbox group under "PROGRESS" label:

    * Select All, Start, First quartile, Midpoint, Third quartile, Complete
    
* "Select All" toggles all checkboxes
* Checked items appear as removable tags/chips below
